name: Quantum Hardware Stack Map
summary: A layered picture of the physical stack beneath a quantum experiment: qubit device, control electronics, environment, calibration, and software orchestration.
whenToUseIt: Use it when a discussion about quantum hardware becomes too abstract and you need to expose the real engineering layers that make the device work.
steps: Map the control and readout electronics or optics.
Add the environmental infrastructure such as vacuum or cryogenics.
Connect the stack to the software and experiment interface.
example: A superconducting platform only makes sense when the chip, microwave lines, cryostat, amplifiers, calibration software, and cloud interface are seen as one system rather than separate topics.
